---
title: "PQAR Statistical Analysis"
author: "Rafaela Drake"
date: "2026-06-12"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r}
#loading data (MIDUS Study)
getwd()
midus_data <-readRDS("~/PQAR 2026/Research Project/midus_cancer_aging_analysis_dataset.rds")
View(midus_data)
dim(midus_data)

colnames(midus_data)

#Install and load packages: tidyverse, gtsummary, flextable, broom, officer, mice, corrr, tibble, misty
library(tidyverse)
library(gtsummary)
library(flextable)
library(broom)
library(officer)
library(mice)
library(corrr)
library(tibble)
library(misty)
library(Hmisc) #corr
library(kableExtra)



#package used for Latex (knitting to PDF for Mac)
library(tinytex)

#data from each column (variable)
univariate_stats <- midus_data %>% select(grimage2:efficacy_STD)
#adds label of what summary value is produced (dependent on data type)
uni_labs <- list(
  grimage2        ~ "GrimAge2 (Epigenetic Age), mean (SD)",
  dunedinpace     ~ "DunedinPACE (Pace of Aging), mean (SD)",
  bmi             ~ "Body Mass Index (BMI), mean (SD)",
  age             ~ "Chronological Age (years), mean (SD)",
  sex             ~ "Sex, No. (%)",
  race_eth        ~ "Race/Ethnicity, No. (%)",
  marital         ~ "Marital Status, No. (%)",
  education       ~ "Educational Attainment, No. (%)",
  income          ~ "Annual Household Income, mean (SD)",
  num_chron       ~ "Number of Chronic Conditions, mean (SD)",
  smoking         ~ "Smoking Status, No. (%)",
  alcohol         ~ "Alcohol Consumption, No. (%)",
  famcancer       ~ "Family History of Cancer, No. (%)",
  cancer_history  ~ "Personal Cancer History, mean (SD)",
  cancerrisk      ~ "Perceived Cancer Risk, mean (SD)",
  efficacy        ~ "Cancer Prevention Self-Efficacy, mean (SD)",
  grimage2_STD    ~ "GrimAge2 (Standardized), mean (SD)",
  dunedinpace_STD ~ "DunedinPACE (Standardized), mean (SD)",
  cancerrisk_STD  ~ "Perceived Cancer Risk (Standardized), mean (SD)",
  efficacy_STD    ~ "Cancer Prevention Self-Efficacy (Standardized), mean (SD)"
)
#tblsummary() function to calculate values and make them into a table, digits = 3
univariate_stats_table <- univariate_stats %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.factor), ~ fct_explicit_na(., "Missing"))) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    statistic = list(
      all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
      all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"
    ),
    missing_text = "Missing",
    digits = all_continuous() ~ 1,
    label = uni_labs
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("Table I. Univariate Statistics on MIDUS II Data")
#view table
univariate_stats_table

univariate_stats_table %>%
  as_gt() %>%
  gt::gtsave("univariate_stats_table.docx")
dim(midus_data)

# - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - CORRELATION MATRIX - - - - - - -

#ADD
#include p-values (add.p)
#function add overall within table 

#Table 4: Zero-Order Correlation Matrix -> perceived cancer risk unadjusted relationships between variables with #emphasis on outcomes and exposure
cor_vars <- midus_data %>%
  select(grimage2, dunedinpace, age, num_chron, cancerrisk_STD, efficacy)

#Convert to a matrix
vars_matrix <- as.matrix(cor_vars)

#Correlation matrix using Hmisc
correlation_results <- Hmisc::rcorr(vars_matrix, type=c("pearson"))
#extract correlation matrix
r_matrix <- correlation_results$r
#extract p-values
p_matrix <- correlation_results$P

#adding starts for p-values and keep lower triagnle
r_formatted <- format(round(r_matrix, 3), nsmall = 3) #issue
stars <- ifelse(p_matrix <0.001, "***",
                ifelse(p_matrix < 0.01, "**",
                       ifelse(p_matrix <0.05, "*", "")))
#combined
final_cor_matrix <- matrix(paste0(r_formatted, stars), nrow = nrow(r_matrix))
rownames(final_cor_matrix) <- rownames(r_matrix)
colnames(final_cor_matrix) <- colnames(r_matrix)

#remove self-correlations
diag(final_cor_matrix) <- ""

#only keep lower triangle
final_cor_matrix[upper.tri(final_cor_matrix)] <- ""

#convert to df and format row names as a separate column
final_df <- as.data.frame(final_cor_matrix)
final_df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(final_df, var = "Variable")

#Professional variable labels
#vector mapping old names to new labels
clean_labels <- c(
  "grimage2"        = "GrimAge2",
  "dunedinpace"     = "DunedinPACE",
  "age"             = "Age",
  "num_chron"       = "Number of chronic conditions",
  "cancerrisk_STD"  = "Perceived cancer risk z-score",
  "efficacy"        = "Self-efficacy"
)

#rename in final df
final_df$Variable <- clean_labels[final_df$Variable]
#add numbers to row names
final_df$Variable <- paste0(1:nrow(final_df), ". ", final_df$Variable) 
#change column headers to numbers (corresponding with row numbering)
colnames(final_df)[2:ncol(final_df)] <- 1:(ncol(final_df) - 1)


#formatting (flextable)
ft <- flextable(final_df) %>%
  #set header labels
  set_header_labels(
    Variable = "Variable",
    grimage2 = "1",
    dunedinpace = "2",
    age = "3",
    num_chron = "4",
    cancerrisk_STD = "5",
    efficacy = "6") %>%
  theme_booktabs() %>% #APA
  bold(part = "header") %>%
  autofit() %>%
  fit_to_width(max_width = 6.5, unit = "in") %>%
  align(j = 1, align = "left", part = "all") %>% 
  align(j = 2:ncol(final_df), align = "center", part = "all") %>%
  # Add a table title
  add_header_lines("1.1. Table 3. Zero-order correlations among epigenetic aging measures, perceived cancer risk, age, self-efficacy, and chronic conditions.") %>%
  #footer with stars info
  add_footer_lines("* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001") %>%
  font(fontname = "Arial", part = "all")

ft

#export
save_as_docx(ft, path = "Table_4_Correlation_Matrix.docx")



      
      
      
      

#- - - - - - OLD
Correlation_Matrix_fmt <- Correlation_Matrix %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "", formatC(., format = "f", digits = 3))))

flextable(Correlation_Matrix_fmt) %>%
  autofit() %>%
  save_as_docx(path = "Table_4_Correlation_Matrix.docx")

# - - - - - - - - - - -MULTIPLE IMPUTATION- - - -- - - - - - 
# Conducting multiple imputation 

# Subsetting the data to include the two standardized outcomes, 
# the standardized exposure, the moderator and the 11 covariates
#15 vars total 
midus_vars <- midus_data %>% 
              select("income", "num_chron", "famcancer", "efficacy",
                     "marital", "education", "grimage2_STD", "dunedinpace_STD",
                     "cancerrisk_STD", "race_eth", "age", "sex",
                     "smoking", "alcohol", "bmi") %>%
  # Categorical variables should be coded as factors in order to invoke polyreg
  mutate(
      race_eth = factor(race_eth),
      alcohol = factor(alcohol),
      sex = factor(sex),
      education = factor(education, ordered = TRUE),
      smoking = factor(smoking),
      marital = factor(marital),
      famcancer = factor(famcancer)
    )
         

# Create a clean character vector tracking all variables in our target analysis subset
analysis_variables <- names(midus_vars)

md.pattern(midus_data) # Counting number of NA
# Pattern summary: 1087 rows of 1156 that are complete. 

md.pairs(midus_data) # Determining four missing patterns 
# Looking at mr / rm values helps understand which values have overlapping data
# to make them eligible for calculating imputations. 

#missing summary function 
missing_summary <- function(data, vars) {
  data %>%
    summarise(
      across(
        all_of(vars), #across all the given vars
        ~ mean(is.na(.x)) * 100 # percentage of variables w/ missing values
      )
    ) %>% #create a data set w/ names - variable names, values - percent missing
    pivot_longer(
      cols = everything(),
      names_to = "variable",
      values_to = "percent_missing"
    ) %>%
  arrange(desc(percent_missing)) #arrange output in descending over 
}


missing_summary(midus_data, analysis_variables)

#predictor matrix 
pred <- mice(midus_vars, maxit = 0)$predictorMatrix 

# Creating a methods object
midus_methods <- make.method(midus_vars) 
midus_methods # Looking to check method types for categorical vars

# Assigning new methods to categorical variables
midus_methods["education"] <- "polr" # changed this from "polyreg" as education is an ordered categorical variable
#polyreg used for categorical values with 2+ categories
midus_methods["famcancer"] <- "logreg" #logreg for nominal categorical values
midus_methods["marital"] <- "polyreg"
midus_methods # Checking to ensure variables are updated

imp <- mice(
  midus_vars, # data: what we want to impute
  m = 10, # number of imputations to perform
  seed = 0864,
  printFlag = FALSE,
  method = midus_methods
)

# Extract the first completed imputed dataset
df_mice_completed <- complete(imp, action = 1)
df_mice_completed


# Re-run missing summary on the complete dataset to verify all missingness dropped to 0%
missing_summary(df_mice_completed, analysis_variables)





```


```{r regression}
#Additional: kableextra library used
#subset by race: non-Hispanic Other
other_pop <- df_mice_completed[df_mice_completed$race_eth == "non-Hispanic Other", ]

#regression of grimage2_STD
m1_nonh_grim <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD + age + sex + marital,
                      data = other_pop)
m1_nonh_grim

m2_nonh_grim <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD + education + income,
                      data = other_pop)

m3_nonh_grim <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD + num_chron + famcancer,
                      data = other_pop)

m4_nonh_grim <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD + efficacy,
                      data = other_pop)

m5_nonh_grim <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD + smoking + alcohol,
                      data = other_pop)

#Table of results
bind_rows(
  tidy(m1_nonh_grim) %>% mutate(model = "Model 1 GrimAge2 STD"),
  tidy(m2_nonh_grim) %>% mutate(model = "Model 2 GrimAge2 STD"),
  tidy(m3_nonh_grim) %>% mutate(model = "Model 3 GrimAge2 STD"),
  tidy(m4_nonh_grim) %>% mutate(model = "Model 4 GrimAge2 STD"),
  tidy(m5_nonh_grim) %>% mutate(model = "Model 5 GrimAge2 STD")
) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  select(model, term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value) %>%
  kable(caption = "GrimAge2 and Cancer Risk Regression Results for Non-Hispanic Other")

#regression of dunedinpace_STD
m1_nonh_dune <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD + age + sex + marital,
                      data = other_pop)

m2_nonh_dune <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ dunedinpace_STD + education + income,
                      data = other_pop)

m3_nonh_dune <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ dunedinpace_STD + num_chron + famcancer,
                      data = other_pop)

m4_nonh_dune <- lm(grimage2_STD ~ dunedinpace_STD + efficacy,
                      data = other_pop)

m5_nonh_duned <- lm(dunedinpace_STD ~ cancerrisk_STD + smoking + alcohol,
                      data = other_pop)

#Table of results
bind_rows(
  tidy(m1_nonh_duned) %>% mutate(model = "Model 1 DunedinPace STD"),
  tidy(m2_nonh_duned) %>% mutate(model = "Model 2 DunedinPACE STD"),
  tidy(m3_nonh_duned) %>% mutate(model = "Model 3 DunedinPACE STD"),
  tidy(m4_nonh_duned) %>% mutate(model = "Model 4 DunedinPACE STD"),
  tidy(m5_nonh_duned) %>% mutate(model = "Model 5 DunedinPACE STD")
) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  select(model, term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value) %>%
  kable(caption = "DunedinPACE and Cancer Risk Regression Results for Non-Hispanic Other")

```
